text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, especially in tech. The real driver for your names is the looming "semiconductor bear market" talk, directly impacting your core chip holdings.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are flashing deep red. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning these drawdowns are amplified, especially on margin. SMH (-10.6%) also needs attention given the chip sector news.

Watch: Keep a very tight eye on those SOXL and SPAL stops. Also, watch for any broader tech weakness that could drag down QQQ and QQQI further.

Bottom line: Your heavy chip concentration on margin is facing significant headwinds; risk management is paramount right now.
plays: Your SOXL is down 44%. The default stop at 200 is too loose given the chip sector news; consider tightening it significantly or cutting losses.
SPAL is also down over 41%. Re-evaluate its stop at 21.38; leveraged ETFs decay and move fast, making tight risk management crucial.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The overall market sentiment for chips is negative, making new long positions in the sector risky.
